claims.text: On 25 June 2026 a magnitude 7.5 earthquake struck 16 km SW of Moron in northern Venezuela, west of Caracas.
The USGS is assessing earthquake-triggered landslide hazards generated by the 2026 Venezuela earthquake sequence.

As of 1 July 2026, nearly 2,000 deaths have been confirmed and more than 6,400 people rescued; 1.8 million people are in need of humanitarian assistance including 680,000 children across six affected states, with around 1,000 buildings and over 400 schools damaged and more than 600 aftershocks recorded since the initial quakes.

description: A magnitude 7.5 earthquake struck northern Venezuela west of Caracas on 25 June 2026, 39 seconds after a magnitude 7.2 foreshock in the same sequence. The USGS assessed that high casualties and widespread damage are probable. As the response enters its second week, the UN reports the death toll and humanitarian needs continuing to climb.

impact.summary: As of 1 July 2026, the UN reported nearly 2,000 confirmed deaths and more than 6,400 people rescued, with 1.8 million people in need of humanitarian assistance including 680,000 children across six affected states. Around 1,000 buildings and over 400 schools have been damaged, and more than 600 aftershocks recorded.

location.region: Near Morón / San Felipe, northern Venezuela (west of Caracas)

name: Venezuela M7.5 Earthquake Doublet
